Shanghai Pharmaceuticals Holding Co ( (HK:2607) ) has provided an update.

Shanghai Pharmaceuticals Holding Co. has signed a partnership agreement to participate in the establishment of the Shanghai Biomedical Phase II Fund, formalizing plans first announced on 18 July 2026. data.title: China Agri-Products Exchange, Wang On Group Seal RMB64 Million Sale-Leaseback with Haier
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Agri-Products Exchange Limited ( (HK:0149) ) has issued an announcement.

China Agri-Products Exchange Limited and its parent Wang On Group Limited have entered into a sale and leaseback arrangement through their subsidiaries Qinzhou Hongjin and Kaifeng Hongjin with Haier as lessor, involving assets sold for RMB64 million, or about HK$73.81 million. The price was set after arm’s-length negotiations, referencing asset costs and an acceptable loan-to-value ratio, and will be paid within 10 business days once specified conditions, including completion of equity pledge registrations by Henan Gangan and Crown Fortress in favour of Haier, are satisfied.

The transaction qualifies as a discloseable transaction for both companies under Hong Kong Listing Rules, as the highest applicable percentage ratio exceeds 5% but is below 25%. This financing move underscores the group’s use of asset-backed structures to unlock liquidity while retaining operational use of the assets, which may bolster its capital flexibility and support ongoing operations without triggering more stringent shareholder approval requirements.

More about China Agri-Products Exchange Limited

China Agri-Products Exchange Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates within the agricultural products and related exchange sector, focusing on the development and operation of agri-product trading and logistics platforms in mainland China. It is affiliated with Wang On Group Limited, which has diversified interests and uses project companies such as Qinzhou Hongjin and Kaifeng Hongjin to hold and manage specific agricultural market assets.

data.title: Lens Technology Signs Intel Memorandum to Explore TGV and AI Hardware Collaboration
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Lens Technology Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6613) ) just unveiled an update.

Lens Technology’s wholly owned subsidiary Lens International (HK) Limited has signed a memorandum of understanding with Intel Corporation to explore cooperation in through-glass via advanced packaging technology. The discussions will cover processes such as glass substrate via formation, high-precision laser processing and metal-filled via deposition, with any concrete engineering validation or volume production to be governed by separate agreements.

Beyond TGV, the parties plan to evaluate potential collaboration in structural components and modules for AI PCs, high-reliability thermal solutions for data center servers and hardware for embodied AI robotics and edge computing. While most provisions of the memorandum are non-binding and effective for one year, Lens Technology’s board expects the framework to deepen its strategic ties with a major semiconductor player, potentially enhancing its role in advanced packaging and AI-related hardware supply chains once specific agreements are reached.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6613) stock is a Buy
with a HK$38.00 price target.

see the HK:6613 Stock Forecast page.

More about Lens Technology Co. Ltd. Class H

Lens Technology Co., Ltd. is a China-based manufacturer specializing in precision glass components and advanced processing technologies for electronics, including through-glass via (TGV) and multi-layer interconnection processes. The company serves global technology and semiconductor supply chains, supplying structural and functional components used in devices such as PCs, data center servers and industrial intelligent equipment.

Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:2521) ) has shared an update.

Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings Limited announced a significant change in its shareholder structure after Prosperity Cleanness Investment Holdings Limited sold 175,476,150 shares, equal to about 9% of the company’s issued share capital. Following this disposal, Prosperity Cleanness’ stake fell from roughly 30.1% to 21.1%, meaning it no longer qualifies as a controlling shareholder under Hong Kong listing rules.

Prosperity Cleanness is wholly owned by co-chairman and executive director Li Chenghua, so the transaction reduces his effective control over the company while leaving him in a key management position. The company advised shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when dealing in its shares, signaling that the shift in ownership could affect perceptions of control, governance dynamics and future strategic decisions.

More about Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings Limited

data.title: China Power flags up to 57% profit drop on weaker wind and solar, softer coal demand
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Power International Development ( (HK:2380) ) has issued an announcement.

China Power International Development warned that profit attributable to ordinary shareholders for the first half of 2026 is expected to fall to about RMB1.1 billion to RMB1.4 billion, a drop of roughly 45% to 57% from a year earlier. The company cited weaker wind and solar resources, heightened tariff volatility in China’s competitive power market and lower coal-fired electricity sales as key drivers of the earnings decline.

Management said strong rainfall boosted hydropower output at subsidiary SPIC Hydropower, delivering a significant profit increase that partially offset the shortfall in other segments. The figures are based on unaudited internal accounts, and the company plans to release its formal interim results in late August 2026, while advising shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when trading its shares.

Beijing Shougang LanzaTech Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2553) ) has shared an announcement.

Beijing Shougang LanzaTech Technology Co., Ltd. has formalized a governance change under its Articles of Association, eliminating its Supervisory Committee and transferring its oversight functions and powers to the Audit Committee. The move follows shareholder approval in 2024 and came into effect after the company completed registration of the change with the State Administration for Market Regulation on July 24, 2026.

As a result of this structural shift, former supervisors Mr. Zhao Yucheng, Ms. Xing Fei and Ms. Zhang Ling have ceased to hold their supervisory roles and have indicated they have no disagreements with the company or its board. The board expressed gratitude for their service, signaling an orderly transition in oversight that aligns the company’s governance framework more closely with Hong Kong listing practices and may streamline board-level risk and audit supervision for stakeholders.

More about Beijing Shougang LanzaTech Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Beijing Shougang LanzaTech Technology Co., Ltd. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong. The company operates in the industrial technology and environmental solutions space, leveraging advanced processes and partnerships to serve both domestic and international markets.

Yidu Tech, Inc. ( (HK:2158) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Yidu Tech Inc. has convened its annual general meeting for August 28, 2026 in Beijing, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended March 31, 2026 and vote on a proposed final dividend. The agenda also includes re-election of two independent non-executive directors, renewal of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor, and broad mandates for the board to issue, allot and transfer shares within defined limits, which would give management continued flexibility in capital management and potential future fundraising.

The share-issuance mandate caps new and treasury share issuances at up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital, with an additional buffer linked to shares repurchased under a separate buyback authorization. These resolutions, if passed, will maintain Yidu Tech’s ability to adjust its capital structure in response to market conditions while formalizing board authority over director remuneration and auditor fees, matters of direct interest to shareholders and corporate governance observers.

More about Yidu Tech, Inc.

The company is governed by a board that includes independent non-executive directors and engages international audit firms, reflecting a structure aligned with global capital market practices.

data.title: CSPC Wins China Approval for Long-Acting Schizophrenia Injection
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group ( (HK:1093) ) has shared an update.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group has received marketing approval in mainland China for its independently developed Paliperidone Palmitate Injection, a second-generation long-acting antipsychotic indicated for both acute and maintenance treatment of schizophrenia. Available in multiple strengths, the once-monthly injection is designed to deliver rapid onset without supplemental oral medication, improving adherence and reducing relapse and rehospitalisation rates for patients with poor treatment compliance.

The product has been recommended in leading domestic and international clinical guidelines, including the 2025 Chinese Guidelines for the Prevention and Treatment of Schizophrenia, underscoring its clinical and pharmacoeconomic value. This approval expands CSPC’s psychiatric and neurological portfolio and strengthens its strategic position in the central nervous system drug market, as the company signals ongoing investment in high-value therapies to broaden treatment options for patients.

Aluminum Corporation of China Limited announced that its controlling shareholder, Chinalco, and concerted parties plan to increase their holdings of the company’s A and H shares by between RMB1 billion and RMB2 billion over up to twelve months, capped at 2% of total share capital. data.title: China Power’s June Hydropower Surge Fails to Offset First-Half Sales Dip
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Power International Development ( (HK:2380) ) has provided an announcement.

China Power International Development reported that group electricity sales in June 2026 rose 1.45% year on year to 11.46 million MWh, driven largely by strong hydropower output, while total sales for the first half slipped 0.81% to 62.03 million MWh. The shift reflects robust growth in hydropower generation, particularly in Hunan and Guizhou, contrasted with declines in wind, coal-fired and some photovoltaic output, signaling an ongoing transition in the company’s generation mix.

Sales from wholly owned and controlled hydropower plants surged 52% in the first half, offsetting weaker contributions from coal-fired power, which fell nearly 11%, and wind power, which dropped about 14%. Major associates and joint ventures also recorded lower electricity sales, indicating broader softness in traditional and some renewable segments, a development that may weigh on overall utilization rates and underscores the company’s increasing reliance on hydropower assets for growth.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2380) stock is a Hold
with a HK$3.30 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Power International Development stock,
see the HK:2380 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Power International Development

China Power International Development is a Hong Kong-incorporated power producer focused on generating and selling electricity across mainland China and selected overseas markets. Its diversified portfolio spans hydropower, wind, photovoltaic, coal-fired, natural gas and environmental power plants, with a notable regional presence in provinces such as Hunan, Guizhou, Sichuan and Guangxi.

data.title: KFM Kingdom Holdings Signs Three-Year Connected Contract Manufacturing Deal
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from KFM Kingdom Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:3816) ).

KFM Kingdom Holdings has entered into a three-year contract manufacturing agreement between its subsidiary KPP(SZ) and PRC-based company HHY, effective from 24 July 2026. Under the deal, KPP(SZ) will manufacture and supply products to HHY, with pricing, quantities, specifications, and delivery details to be negotiated separately.

The arrangement constitutes a continuing connected transaction because HHY is 70% indirectly owned by Jack Sun, son of chairman and executive director Peter Sun, making HHY an associate of a connected person under Hong Kong listing rules. As the relevant transaction ratios exceed 0.1% but remain below 5%, the deal is a non-exempt connected transaction subject to disclosure and annual review, but it does not require independent shareholders’ approval, signaling manageable compliance obligations for the company and clarifying governance treatment for investors.

More about KFM Kingdom Holdings Ltd.

data.description:

Virtual Mind Holding Company Limited ( (HK:1520) ) has issued an update.

Virtual Mind Holding Company Limited has scheduled an extraordinary general meeting to be held in Hong Kong on 13 August 2026 at 11:00 a.m., providing shareholders with an opportunity to attend and vote on corporate matters. The meeting will take place at World-Wide House in Central, with the record date set for the same day.

To determine shareholders’ eligibility to participate in the meeting, the company will close its register of members from 10 to 13 August 2026, inclusive. Share transfer documents must be lodged with the Hong Kong branch share registrar by 4:30 p.m. on 7 August 2026, a procedural step that ensures only duly registered shareholders can exercise their voting rights at the extraordinary general meeting.

More about Virtual Mind Holding Company Limited

Virtual Mind Holding Company Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 1520, operates with a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors. The company is headquartered in Hong Kong and maintains a branch share registrar and transfer office in the city for handling its shareholder-related matters.

OSL Group Limited ( (HK:0863) ) has provided an announcement.

OSL Group Limited has announced a change in its corporate governance team, appointing Mr. Ko Sebastian Yat Fung as company secretary and authorised representative in Hong Kong effective 24 July 2026. Mr. Ko, who joined the group in April 2026 and leads its legal team for international businesses, brings over 15 years of legal experience and multiple professional qualifications, while the board expressed its appreciation for outgoing company secretary Ms. Kuo Yuen Fan’s contributions and signalled continuity in regulatory and compliance oversight.

The appointment underscores OSL’s focus on strengthening its legal and compliance capabilities as it manages international operations and ongoing obligations under Hong Kong listing rules. Investors and other stakeholders can interpret the move as an effort to reinforce governance infrastructure and ensure robust handling of regulatory processes, potentially supporting the group’s strategic execution and risk management as its business evolves.
